summary: Expanded camera static data coverage by moving many camera globals, literals, descriptors, callbacks, and small structs into src/melee/cm/camera.static.h and cm/types.h. The PR also corrected camera prototype signatures for several no-argument void functions and adjusted cm_803BCB64 users from HSD_CObjDesc.perspective.aspect to HSD_CameraDescPerspective.aspect after changing the static descriptor type. A decomp.dev bot report noted a regression for Camera_8002A768, so the matching impact was mixed despite the header/static-data progress.
what_changed: Added baselib forward and wobj includes to camera.static.h so camera static descriptors can reference HSD_WObjDesc and camera object descriptor types directly.

Reconstructed and initialized multiple camera static symbols in camera.static.h, including CameraModeCallbacks cm_803BCB18, HSD_WObjDesc cm_803BCB3C/cm_803BCB50, HSD_CameraDescPerspective cm_803BCB64, CameraFixednessMult cm_803BCB9C, assertion/message strings, Vec3 rodata constants, sbss/sdata entries, and many sdata2 float/double constants.
Changed cm_803BCB64 from HSD_CObjDesc to HSD_CameraDescPerspective, which required camera.c uses to access cm_803BCB64.aspect instead of cm_803BCB64.perspective.aspect.
Added CameraModeCallbacks and CameraFixednessMult struct definitions to src/melee/cm/types.h.
Added forward declarations for CameraModeCallbacks and CameraFixednessMult in src/melee/cm/forward.h.

Refined several camera.h declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void no-argument prototypes, including Camera_8002B3D4, Camera_8002C908, Camera_8002CDDC, Camera_8002D318, Camera_8002D85C, Camera_8002DDC4, and Camera_8002E490.
Left camera.static.h inclusion in db_2253.c as a commented-out include, while retaining cm/camera.h inclusion.
smart_moves: Grouped reconstructed camera statics by section comments such as .bss, .data, .rodata, .sbss, .sdata, and sdata2, making address-to-section reasoning easier for later decomp work.
Used concrete initialized descriptor data for camera WObj and perspective camera descriptors instead of leaving the global as an opaque HSD_CObjDesc.
Propagated the descriptor type change into camera.c field accesses rather than forcing the old nested perspective layout.
Converted known no-argument callback targets from unknown prototypes to explicit void signatures, which improves function pointer and callback table documentation.
Kept uncertain data commented instead of pretending it was fully solved, e.g. the CameraDebugMode bss symbol, a possible jump table, and a note that cm_804D6468 may require size 8.
decomp_lessons: When reconstructing static headers, section placement and address comments are useful documentation, but type/layout changes can affect codegen in consumers and should be checked against matching reports.

Changing a global descriptor from a wrapper type to a more specific descriptor type can simplify field access, but it may alter generated code; compare before/after for functions that take addresses of nested fields.
Callback tables are good evidence for tightening function prototypes: if entries are called as no-arg void callbacks, update both the table type and declarations together.
Do not overcommit uncertain static data. Commented placeholders for unresolved bss globals or jump tables are safer than incorrect live definitions.
Literal pools in sdata2 often need exact float/double spelling and array grouping to preserve ordering and alignment; record addresses and values together.
naming_conventions: Camera statics follow the module/address pattern cm_803Bxxxx for data/rodata and cm_804Dxxxx for small data/small bss.
New camera-specific struct names use descriptive PascalCase: CameraModeCallbacks and CameraFixednessMult.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic struct fields remained offset-based, such as x0, x4, x8, xC, and x10 in CameraFixednessMult.
Known camera functions continue using address-based names like Camera_8002A768 and Camera_8002E490 until semantic names are known.
Assertion/message labels used either cm_803Bxxxx names or existing lbl_803Bxxxx style where semantic ownership was not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reconstructed static initializers for camera descriptors, vectors, strings, scalar constants, and callback arrays directly in camera.static.h to match section contents.
Used exact-looking float constants such as 0.017453292f, 1.5707964f, 3.4028235E+38f, and paired float arrays for sdata2 literals.
Adjusted field access to match the selected HSD_CameraDescPerspective layout: cm_803BCB64.aspect.
Added concrete void prototypes for callback-table functions to better match calls and function pointer usage.
Potential jump table data was not enabled; it remained commented out, implying the author avoided introducing risky unmatched data without stronger evidence.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported a matching regression: `main/melee/cm/camera | Camera_8002A768` changed by -120 bytes and dropped to 65.62%.
The bot report suggests the cm_803BCB64 type/layout or related static-header changes affected Camera_8002A768 codegen; no human explanation or resolution is available in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching Camera_8002A768 or cm_803BCB64 to see whether the -120 byte matching regression was repaired.
Search for CameraModeCallbacks consumers to determine whether mode_0 through mode_8 should become typed function pointers instead of void*.
Search for CameraFixednessMult usage to replace x0/x4/x8/xC/x10 with semantic field names.
Investigate whether commented CameraDebugMode cm_80453004 and jumptable_803BCC4C were later resolved.
Check whether db_2253.c ever legitimately includes cm/camera.static.h or whether the commented include was only exploratory.
